subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.1. Examine the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Discuss the fourth-generation computers
examplars:
1. Discuss features of fourth generation computers
2. Identify a microchip
3. Explore the architecture of a processor

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.1. Examine the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding in the use of input devices (barcode, scanner, etc.)
examplars:
1. Watch video or picture of input devices e.g. wireless keyboard, mouse and touchscreen in class
2. Demonstrate the use of input devices in a computer laboratory/classroom.
3. Distinguish manual (e.g. keyboard, etc.) and automatic (e.g. barcode readeretc.) input devices.
4 Explore the advantages and disadvantages of input devices
5. Explore areas where different types of input devices are used.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.1. Examine the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Examine the uses of the output devices: graphing plotter, data and multimedia projectors as well as pico projector
examplars:
1. Watch video or pictures of output devices in use.
2. Demonstrate the use of output devices in a computer laboratory/classroom.
3. Explore the advantages and disadvantages of output devices.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.1. Examine the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Examine full-sized external hard drives, hard drive speed, disk caching, Storage portable hard drives, Optical Discs and Drives.
examplars:
1. Identify magnetic storage devices, portable hard drives/Optical Discs and Drives or pictures of these items to class.
2. Discuss the features of hard drives/Optical Disc storage media.
3. Explore the maximum capacities of these storage devices.
4. Explore the different write speeds of these storage devices.
5. Explore the differences in the various Hard Disk Drives (HDD).

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.2. Demonstrate the use of the features of the Windows Desktop
indicator_description: Discover the latest Windows Operating System (Start screen, Use of tiles, Taskbar buttons, Preview thumbnails), temporal peeking into a window on a taskbar
examplars:
1. Show the desktop, tiles, taskbar.
2. Demonstrate how to preview thumbnails
3. Explore the features of the taskbar.
4. Demonstrate how to preview windows on the taskbar.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B7.1.1.2. Demonstrate the use of the features of the Windows Desktop
indicator_description: Practise file management techniques (file & folder management, Users & Accounts)
examplars:
1. Demonstrate file management techniques by following the naming conventions and organising files in folders and subfolders
2. Explore the types and importance of file extensions.
3. Explore different account levels for users of computer systems
4. Explore different permission levels that are applied to files and folders.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Describe and give examples of at least five technology tools for learning in each subject
examplars:
1. Explore the various technology tools that can be used for learning.
NB: Exploration can be done through learners surfing the internet or the teacher guiding them to brainstorm the ICT tools.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of at least three technology tools identified in B7.1.2.1.1.
examplars: 1. Demonstrate the use of a technology tool in groups and present to the whole class how that tool works.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Discuss the benefits of using technology tools in learning.
examplars: 1. Discuss in pairs the benefits of using technology tools in learning (e.g. using spreadsheet to draw graphs)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Examine the negative impact of computers and computer use on the environment
examplars:
1. Observe people who use and work with computers in the community.
2. Visit websites or watch videos/pictures of how computers, including other electronic components, are disposed of.
3. Discuss the impact of computers and computer use on the environment.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Propose environmentally responsible practices that can be used to reduce the negative impact of computers and computer use on the environment
examplars:
1. Discuss how the negative effects identified can be reduced.
2. Evaluate environmentally responsible practices.
3. Propose measures to effectively manage e-waste in a particular environment (e.g. Agbogbloshie).

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Create a component from disposed computer parts.
examplars:
1. Collect disposed computer/electronic parts from the community.
2. Watch a video/picture depicting the recycling of computer parts.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B7.1.3.1. Demonstrate how to apply Health and Safety measures in using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Describe health measures and current regulatory requirements and potential computing-related disorders
examplars:
1. Watch videos on the health hazards of prolonged use of computing devicesor show pictures of bad body postures and other hazards in using computing devices e.g. hearing impairment from loud Public Address (PA) Systems, vision impairment from the monitor, repetitive strain injury, Carpaltunnel syndrome, computer vision syndrome, etc.
2. Identify the health hazards associated with each device.
3. Provide preventive measures regarding the stated health and safety risks.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B7.1.3.1. Demonstrate how to apply Health and Safety measures in using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Describe Safety measures in using ICT tools
examplars:
1. Discuss the danger of spilling liquids on a computer device or on the electronic circuit.
2. Discuss tripping over power cables and touching the negative and positive terminals of electrical wires.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B7.1.3.1. Demonstrate how to apply Health and Safety measures in using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to apply Health and Safety measures in Using ICT Tools / Examine workstation health risk assessment methods
examplars: 1. Explore the risks associated with workstations and how to overcome them (e.g. wrist pains, eye problems, back and neck pains, faulty electrical connections, etc.)

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B7.1.3.1. Demonstrate how to apply Health and Safety measures in using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Explore safety measures at workstations
examplars: 1. Identify measures that will help to eliminate workstation hazards and where they cannot be eliminated, discuss how to minimise the risk. (e.g. evaluating display screen, adjusting the chair for comfort, avoiding potential slips and falls, re-positioning of devices, etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Explain the importance of word processing software
examplars:
1. Discuss the meaning of word processors.
2. Brainstorm to elicit the importance of word processors
3. Brainstorm to elicit examples of some common word processing software packages (e.g. MS-Word, Corel WordPerfect, AbiWord, Google Docs, LibreOffice Writer, Notepad, WordPad, etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to insert, select, delete and move text
examplars:
1. Show projected examples of MS-Word interface with the aid of a computer, projector or pictures.
2. Explore tools for editing in MS Word
3. Explore the use of the overtype or insert option by right-clicking the statusbar.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to find and replace content and undo edited changes
examplars:
1. Make use of the Find and Replace tool in MS-Word under the Home tab
2. Explore the use of the Editing group under the Home tab

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to spell check, carry out content translation, language setting
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the use of the Proofing and Language group under the Reviewtab
2. Show how to use the Language, Spelling & Grammar, Thesaurus andother tools in MS-Word under the Home tab.
NB: This is to help learners gain software knowledge in office applications(word processing) to grasp the concept better.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use text-decoration, change text case, text size and colour
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Font group under the Home tab
2. Demonstrate the use of sentence case, font size, colour and font decoration features in MS-Word
3. Project examples of MS-Word interface to learners with the aid of a projectoror pictures.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Editing)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to align text, indent paragraphs, bullet, line space and shade
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Paragraph group, using the align left, centre, align rightand justified in MS-Word under the Home tab.
2. Explore the use of Bullets, Decrease and Increase Indentation under the Home tab.
3. Identify the use of the Border Button and set line spacing using the dialogueBox Launcher button under the Home tab.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Word (Formatting Text)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to set tabs and apply formatting
examplars:
1. Explore the Tab button to set the centre and right tabs
NB: This is to help learners with software knowledge in office applications (word processing) to grasp the concept better.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Editing): Introduction to PowerPoint.
indicator_description: Explain the importance of presentation software
examplars:
1. Discuss the meaning of presentation software.
2. Discuss the benefits of using presentation software.
3. Brainstorm to elicit the names of some common presentation software packages (e.g. MS-PowerPoint, Corel Presentation, Adobe persuasion, Google Slides, Keynotes etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Editing): Introduction to PowerPoint.
indicator_description: Explore features of MS-PowerPoint interface.
examplars:
1. Show examples of MS-PowerPoint interface with the aid of a projector or pictures.
2. Explore MS-PowerPoint themes and templates
3. Explore the use of the Proofing and Language group under the Review tab
4. Demonstrate the use of the Language, Spelling & Grammar, Thesaurusand other buttons in MS-PowerPoint under the Review tab

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Editing): Introduction to PowerPoint.
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use Special Characters. Design a 7-slide presentation in MS-PowerPoint using the tools under the Insert ribbon.
examplars:
1. Explore the use of special characters section under the Insert tab underthe symbol group
2. Present a prepared project or exercise using the editing group of the ribbons studied.
3. Use projected examples of a PowerPoint interface with the aid of a projector or pictures
4. Use projected examples of a PowerPoint interface with the aid ofa projector or pictures.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Formatting)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to change text case, text size, text colour and decorate text
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Font group under the Home ribbon.
2. Make use of the sentence case, font size, colour and font decoration features in MS-PowerPoint
3. Project examples of PowerPoint interface to learners with the aid of a projector or pictures.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Formatting)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to align text, indent paragraphs, borders and shades.
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Proofing and Language Sections under the Review ribbon.
2. Use the Language, Spelling & Grammar, Thesaurus and other buttons inMS-PowerPoint under the Review ribbon.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.2.2 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Formatting)
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the Slide Master, design template, and be able to give a 5-slide presentation
examplars:
1. Explore the use of Master Views group under the View ribbon
2. Prepare and present a prepared project or exercise using what has been studied in Indicator I and 2.
NB: This is to help the learners with software knowledge in MS-PowerPoint, Office Applications to grasp the concept well.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use the Spreadsheet (Editing Worksheets)
indicator_description: Explain the importance of electronic spreadsheet
examplars:
1. Discuss the meaning of electronic spreadsheet.
2. Discuss the benefits of using electronic spreadsheet software.
3. Brainstorm to elicit response some common electronic spreadsheet software packages (e.g. MS Excel, Lotus 1-2-3, LibreOffice Calc, GoogleSheets etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use the Spreadsheet (Editing Worksheets)
indicator_description: Explore features of MS-Excel interface
examplars:
1. Show projected examples of MS-Excel interface with the aid of a projectoror pictures.
2. Explore operations of inserting, selecting, deleting and moving data.
3. Demonstrate how to insert, select, delete and move data using a sampledata set.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use the Spreadsheet (Editing Worksheets)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to set the cell datatype (General, Number, Currency, etc.).
examplars:
1. Investigate how to set and modify the cell type of values and text.
2. Enter values, text, dates and time in worksheet cells and change the formatsfor presentation. E.g. General, Number, Currency, Accounting, Dates, Time, etc.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use the Spreadsheet (Editing Worksheets)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use Align Text, Merge & Wrap, Borders and Shades
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to change text alignment (Horizontal & Vertical), mergecells and wrap text.
2. Investigate how to access border & shade features and format the appearance of a worksheet as group work.

subject: Computing
content_standard: Β7.2.3.2. Demonstratehow to format a worksheet
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to adjust margins and set page orientation
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to adjust margins and set page orientation for printing.
2. Perform margin adjustment on different page sizes
3. Explore the display of worksheets in different views as listed on the View tab.

subject: Computing
content_standard: Β7.2.3.2. Demonstratehow to format a worksheet
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to set up a header and a footer.
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to set up header and footer elements.
2. Explore the use of page numbers, current date, time and file name in setting up headers and footers.

subject: Computing
content_standard: Β7.2.3.2. Demonstratehow to format a worksheet
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the Autofill function in MS-Excel worksheet
examplars: 1. Demonstrate the use of the Autofill function e.g. to generate the days of the week, months of the year, set of numbers (e.g. counting numbers, odd numbers, multiplication tables etc.).

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7 2.3.3 Demonstrate how to use spreadsheet formula
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to create formulas
examplars: 1. Create simple formulas starting with the equal sign (=)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Identify the concept of computer networking for global communications
indicator_description: Draw diagrams to illustrate features of the network topologies (Bus, Star, Ring, Mesh)
examplars:
1. Explore key hardware for setting up network systems (such as server, client, hub, switch, cable, etc.).
2. Explain network topologies.
3. Discuss the features of each network topology.
4. Present in groups diagrams of well-elaborated network topologies. NB: Watch any appropriate video on the above

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Identify the concept of computer networking for global communications
indicator_description: Describe types of networks [Personal Area Network (PAN), Local Area Network (LAN), Metropolitan Area Network (MAN), Wide Area Network (WAN)]
examplars:
1. Explain the various types of networks available (e.g. PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN, WLAN, INTERNET, etc.).
2. A Metropolitan Area Network (MAN) connects local networks across a larger geographical region.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.1.1 Identify the concept of computer networking for global communications
indicator_description: Discuss the entrepreneurial opportunities in networking computing devices
examplars:
1. Discuss the benefits and challenges of networking in different environments (school, business, health, etc.).
2. Identify different environments where the various types of networks can be applied
3. Identify the business aspect of networking and how they can be turned intoa lucrative business.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.2.1 Demonstrate the use of Social Networking and Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Identify the various types and uses of Social Media sites
examplars:
1. Illustrate the use of social networking sites such as Facebook, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, etc.
2. Demonstrate the use of microblogging platforms such as Twitter, Tumblr, etc.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.2.1 Demonstrate the use of Social Networking and Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the following features of Electronic mail: Attachment and Address book
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the steps in creating, sending and receiving email
2. Demonstrate replying to and forwarding email
3. Demonstrate, giving reasons for using From:, To: cc:, bcc: and subject features when sending an email.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and means of protection
indicator_description: Discuss the key principles of information security (confidentiality, integrity and availability)
examplars:
1. Research in pairs the key principles of information security.
2. Discuss the three key principles of information security.
3. Research scenarios involving information security

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and means of protection
indicator_description: Explore the legal issues regarding intellectual property rights
examplars:
1. Discuss issues pertaining to copyright (e.g. freeware, shareware, crippleware).
2. Differentiate between the various legal issues mentioned.
3. Discuss the consequences associated with breaking these laws.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and means of protection
indicator_description: Evaluate information security forensic auditing and criminal laws against offenders
examplars:
1. Watch a video of how offenders of data security breach are identified.
2. Discuss the laws protecting data and the applicable sanctions for their breach.
3. Identify some common occurrences of data security breaches that people inthe community overlook and their corresponding sanctions.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.4.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser (Search engine)
indicator_description: Identify the importance of the web in learning [Virtual Learning Environments (VLEs)]
examplars:
1. Explore the importance of VLEs for learning.
2. Allow self-paced learning (E-learning).
3. Create opportunity to learn new skills without having to use a regular classroom.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.4.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser (Search engine)
indicator_description: Explore the use of open learning websites in the classroom
examplars: 1. Explore the uses of open learning websites in the classroom e.g. KhanAcademy, Coursera, Edx, Saylor, etc.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.3.4.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser (Search engine)
indicator_description: Demonstrate the techniques for evaluating web pages (Accuracy, Credibility, Content, Current, Functionality)
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the techniques for evaluating web pages.
2. Accuracy: How true is the information?
3. Credibility: Who wrote the page? Is the person an expert in the subject matter?
4. Content: Is it on the correct subject matter?
5. Current: Is the content up-to-date? When was the last time it was updated?
6. Functionality: Does the site work well?

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.1.1. Show an understanding of the concept of programming
indicator_description: Demonstrate the correct use of programming terminologies
examplars:
1. List the terminologies in alphabetical order or grouping to aid recall
2. Explain each of the terminologies

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.1.1. Show an understanding of the concept of programming
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding in the use of data types (e.g. float, integer, string, char, etc.)
examplars: 1. Develop key questions around daily activities to identify the data type. Example: The first name of your best friend is written as a string data type.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.1.1. Show an understanding of the concept of programming
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of constants and variables used in programming
examplars:
1. Show how constants and variables are used in programming.
2. Discuss the benefits of using variables instead of constants.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.2.1. Analyse the correct step-by-step procedure in solving any real-world problem
indicator_description: Understand the use of sequence, selection and iteration in writing a programme.
examplars:
1. Write numbers (1-10) in an orderly arrangement to represent sequence. Write your itinerary for a day in a logical order (Sequence).
NB: The above should be linear with no branching statements.
2. Present a case study that has more than one option to choose from and still achieve the same outcome with any option chosen.
3. Develop a solution to a problem which uses iteration to control theflow of the programme (iteration).
NB: Programs such as lightbot could be used for practical lessons.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.2.1. Analyse the correct step-by-step procedure in solving any real-world problem
indicator_description: Perform a linear search
examplars:
1. Locate a given value position out of listed values.
2. Arrange some given values or data in increasing and decreasing order.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.3.1 Discuss Robot Intelligence Concepts
indicator_description: Review the various applications of robotic machines insociety
examplars:
1. State the applications and uses of robots in society (e.g. manufacturing, health, education, assembling and packing, transport, surgery, laboratory research, mass production of consumer and industrial goods, taking pictures,etc.)
2. Explore prospects and challenges of using robots in various operations

subject: Computing
content_standard: B7.4.4.1. Discuss Artificial intelligence concepts
indicator_description: Discuss the application of various areas of artificial intelligence
examplars:
1. Compare the key technologies: machine learning, Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Reality, Augmented reality, Gamification, Deep Learning, Artificial Data Mining and Analytics.
2. Discuss the uses and importance of Artificial Intelligence (Al) to society.
3. Watch video/picture of the use of Al in society (intelligent robots).

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Identify parts a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Discuss the fifth generation of computers with emphasis of on quantum computing
examplars:
1. Discuss the features of the fifth-generation computers.
2. Describe quantum computing using the Google operational quantum computing called "Sycamore".
3. Discuss parallel processing hardware and Artificial Intelligence (Al) software.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Identify parts a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of direct data entry devices
examplars:
1. Identify Graphic tablet, Magnetic card reader, optical card reader, QR code reader, Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) Readers from video or pictures.
2. Explore features of these input devices.
3. Explore how these input devices work in real life situations.
4. Generate QR codes and link them to specific websites.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Examine the uses of the output devices: Braille printers, Impact, Inkjet, Thermal, Wax, 3D printers
examplars:
1. Identify Braille printers, Impact, Inkjet, Thermal, Wax, 3D printersfrom pictures or videos.
2. Explore the features of these output devices.
3. Explore how these output devices work in real life situations.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.1. Identify parts of a computer and technology tools
indicator_description: Describe storage devices: Flash Memory Storage Systems, Embedded Flash Memory Cards and Readers, USB Flash Drives, Solid State Drives and Hybrid hard drives
examplars:
1. Illustrate the use of Flash Memory Storage Systems, Embedded Flash Memory, Flash Memory Cards and Readers, USB Flash Drives, Solid State Drives and Hybrid hard drives.
2. Discuss the features of Flash Memory Storage Systems, Embedded Flash Memory Flash Memory Cards and Readers.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.2. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop features.
indicator_description: Explore the use of the Charms bar
examplars:
1. Identify the icons in the Charms bar
2. Describe features of the Charms bar icons

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B8.1.1.2. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop features.
indicator_description: Practise file management techniques (Drive Management)
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the file management techniques such as defragmentation, compression of files, etc.
2. Explore ways of partitioning a hard disk.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Discuss technologies that help to improve computer accessibility (adaptive and assistive technologies)
examplars:
1. Identify the categories of people with special needs.
2. Discuss technologies that can be used to help people with special needs (e.g. Computer software and hardware such as voice recognition programs, screen readers, and screen enlargement applications, to help people with mobility and sensory impairments use computers and mobile devices, etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Describe how portable computing devices affect our everyday lives
examplars:
1. Discuss portable computing devices that we use daily e.g. mobile phones,smart watches, etc.
2. Describe how these devices affect our daily lives.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Explain the issues associated with online services (e.g. social media, wikis, blogs, etc.)
examplars:
1. Identify the online services that learners normally use or have access to.
2. Evaluate issues that are associated with online service delivery.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B8.1.3.1. Demonstrate How to Apply Health and Safety measures in Using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Discuss health issues at workstations
examplars:
1. Discuss the importance of taking regular breaks from bulk work (possibly after every hour).
2. Discuss the adoption of good posture while at the computer
3. Discuss the use of document holders to avoid having to lean over and bendyour neck while looking at paperwork.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B8.1.3.1. Demonstrate How to Apply Health and Safety measures in Using ICT Tools
indicator_description: Discuss safety measures in risk reduction at workstations
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the use of appropriate volumes when using speakers and earpieces.
2. Demonstrate the use of screen protectors/spectacles to control the amount of light received by our eyes.
3. Illustrate how not to overload electric sockets but use trailing multi-socket units rather than plug adapters.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (tables and hyperlink pages)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to create a table and hyperlinks
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Tables group under the Insert tab
2. Create tables, columns and resize them in MS-Word
3. Explore the use of hyperlinks to create non-linear presentations.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (tables and hyperlink pages)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to merge, split, add formula, borders and shades
examplars:
1. Explore merging, splitting, adding formulas, borders and shades in MS-Wordunder the Insert tab.
2. Explore the use of the bullets; decrease and increase indentation under the Home tab.
3. Explore the use of the Border Button and set line spacing (e.g. explore theuse of the dialogue Box Launcher button under the Home tab)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (tables and hyperlink pages)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to format a page (e.g. page adjustment, inserting header and footer, page numbers, breaks and orientations)
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to format pages by adjusting the header, footer, pagenumbers, and page orientation
NB. This is to help the learners with software knowledge in office applications (word processing) to grasp the concept better.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B82.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add pictures, screenshot and edit and format pictures
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Images Group under the Insert tab
2. Demonstrate the use of ClipArt, Photo Album and Screenshot
3. Project examples of the PowerPoint interface to learners with the aid of a projector or pictures.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B82.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add a drawing canvas, shapes, and also edit, format and add text to shapes
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Illustrations group under the Insert tab
2. Illustrate the use of Shapes and SmartArt
3. Explore the use of the drawing canvas to group shapes.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B82.2.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add text to shapes and arrangeshapes.
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Format Ribbon once a shape is selected
2. Explore the editing features of the Insert Shapes and Shape Styles.
3. Present a prepared project or exercise using what has been studied in Indicator I and 2.
NB: This is to help the learners with software knowledge in MS PowerPoint, Office Applications to grasp the concept well.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Explain the importance of desktop publishing software (DTP)
examplars:
1. Discuss the meaning of desktop publishing software.
2. Brainstorm to elicit the importance of DTP.
3. Brainstorm to elicit responses of some DTP packages. (e.g. MS-Publisher, Adobe InDesign, LibreOffice Draw, QuarkXpress etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Create and save a new document from a blank or pre-designed template
examplars:
1. Open a desktop publishing software (e.g. MS-Publisher).
2. Create a new document from a blank publication
3. Create a new document from a pre-designed template.
4. Save the document with the appropriate name.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the commands in MS-Publisher ribbons
examplars: 1. Working in pairs, explore the use of the commands in a desktop publishingsoftware (e.g. MS-Publisher ribbons: Home, Page Design, Mailings, Review, View)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Change the orientation and margins of a document
examplars: 1. Explore and change the orientation and margins of your document by working pairs.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Add and modify pictures from different sources
examplars: 1. Explore addition and modification of pictures from different sources to your document by working in pairs.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Add and modify text
examplars: 1. Explore addition and modification of text using different font types in your document.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.3.1. Demonstrate how to use MS-Publisher
indicator_description: Create and present a Publisher document (flyer, advertisement, invitation cards, business cards)
examplars:
1. Create a one-page Publisher document e.g. flyer, advertisement, invitationcards, business cards, etc.
2. Present documents to demonstrate creative abilities.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.4.1. Demonstrate How to Use the Spreadsheet (using functions and complexformulas)
indicator_description: Perform operations using functions and Built-in functions
examplars:
1. Enumerate the difference between formulas and functions.
2. Access built-in functions to perform operations on sample data.
3. Demonstrate the use of common spreadsheet functions such as SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, COUNTA, COUNTIF, MAX and MIN.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.4.1. Demonstrate How to Use the Spreadsheet (using functions and complexformulas)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to create complex formulas
examplars: 1. Create complex formulas (e.g. finding percentages, commissions, interestrates, etc.).

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.2.4.1. Demonstrate how to use the Spreadsheet. (using functions and complex formulas)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to copy formulas and references
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the procedure for copying and pasting formulas in a worksheet.
2. Explore how to reference cells and ranges in a worksheet.
3. Demonstrate the use of relative and absolute cell referencing in creating formulas.
4. Explore how to correct common formula errors.
5. Complete a project that involves creating a set of formulas with common functions (e.g. simple interest formula)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.1.1. Identify the concept of computer networking for global communication
indicator_description: Describe the data communication models for networks.
examplars:
1. Explain the Open System Interconnection (OSI) model
2. Identify the different layers in the OSI model.
3. Discuss the purpose or benefits of the layers in ensuring interoperability of different hardware devices.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.1.1. Identify the concept of computer networking for global communication
indicator_description: Describe the Internet, world wide web (www) and Internet Protocol (IP) addresses
examplars:
1. Describe the Internet and the classes of internet addresses.
2. Explain the internet Domain Name Server (DNS), which is equivalent to the function of a phonebook.
3. Distinguish between IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.
4. Explore the difference between internet and world wide web (www).

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.2.1 Demonstrate the use of Social Networking and Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Identify the various types of Social Media sites such as Photo sharing and Video sharing
examplars:
1. Discuss the use of Photo sharing sites such as Instagram, Snapchat, Pinterest etc.
2. Demonstrate the use of video sharing platforms such as YouTube, Facebook Live, Periscope, Vimeo etc.
3. Illustrate the steps involved in attaching a document to an email.
4. Explore the use of the address book as a feature of email.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and security protections
indicator_description: Describe the nature of four major data threats (Interruption, Interception, Modification, Fabrication)
examplars:
1. Watch a video on threats to data security.
2. Discuss the threats that can prevent information from reaching its destination.
3. Discuss the threats that can cause data corruption.
4. Describe the nature of the four major data threats.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and security protections
indicator_description: Map the protection methods to each of the four identified data threats
examplars:
1. Brainstorm the methods of protecting data against the four main threats.
2. Describe the threats to data security and the methods of preventing each threat.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.4.1. Demonstrate the use of a web browser (Search engine)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to effectively search from a web browser.
examplars:
1. Identify effective search techniques (e.g. using search phrases with exactspelling, use of AND, OR, NOT, etc.
2. Demonstrate how to search with any of the techniques or a combination of techniques.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.3.4.1. Demonstrate the use of a web browser (Search engine)
indicator_description: Explore the use of more than one search engine
examplars:
1. Investigate the use of more than one search engine e.g. Ask, Google, yahoo!
2. Explore different search engines with the same search string/terms and observe the outcome
3. Discuss the results of your findings in Exemplar(s): 2 above.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.4.1.1. Show an understanding of the concept of programming
indicator_description: Describe the basic concepts in programming (Constants, Variables, Expressions, Statements /Instructions, logical and arithmetic operators, Operator precedence, etc.)
examplars: 1. Create a table to compare how the same arithmetic notations arerepresented in coding and in classroom mathematics.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.4.2.1. Analyse the correct step-by-step procedure in solving any real-world problem
indicator_description: Apply variables, expressions, assignment statements and operator precedence order (BODMAS rule)
examplars: 1. Compute an expression following the operator precedence order (BODMAS) to exemplify how computers process input data to print out ananswer.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.4.2.1. Analyse the correct step-by-step procedure in solving any real-world problem
indicator_description: Describe and use sequence, selection and iteration statements in a programme.
examplars:
1. Draw the four basic symbols representing programme start-stop, input-output, process and decision.
2. Identify a real case problem in the environment and arrange the symbols to represent a logical step-by-step sequence in solving that problem.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.4.3.1. Discuss Robot Intelligence Concepts
indicator_description: Describe the principles underlying the operation of the components of a robot (Controller Mechanical, Sensors)
examplars:
1. Explain the controller as the "brain" of the robot.
2. Demonstrate understanding of mechanical parts such as motors, pistons, grippers, wheels and gears that make the robot move, grab, turn around orlift (Actuators).
3. Watch video/pictures of the various parts of the robot.
4. Describe how a range of sensors can be used to input data into a computer system
5. Describe how these sensors are used in real-life scenarios

subject: Computing
content_standard: B8.4.4.1 Discuss Artificial Intelligence Concepts
indicator_description: Discuss Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and compareintelligence in humans, animals and machines
examplars:
1. Compare intelligence in humans, animals and machines.
2. Compare the limitations and capabilities of the three intelligences in processing information
3. Discuss the difference between strong and weak artificial intelligence.
4. Discuss hologram science basics and link the application in creating a3-Dimension mixed reality (MR) intelligence.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Identify parts of a Computer and Technology Tools
indicator_description: Discuss the trends in the next generation of computers
examplars:
1. Identify features expected to be seen in the next generation of computers.
2. Describe Google quantum computer (sycamore) and compare its processing power with other supercomputers (focus on processor).

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Identify parts of a Computer and Technology Tools
indicator_description: Examine the concept of Perceptual Computing
examplars: 1. Discuss the features of Perceptual Computing.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Identify parts of a Computer and Technology Tools
indicator_description: Discuss the uses of Output devices such as Wearable Displays, E-Paper, E-Books, Kindle
examplars: 1. Describe Wearable Displays (e.g. Google Glass), E-Paper, E-Books (e.g.Kindle)

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.1 Identify parts of a Computer and Technology Tools
indicator_description: Describe Storage Systems: Network and Cloud Storage Systems, Smart Cards, Holographic Storage
examplars:
1. Discuss Network and Cloud Storage Systems, Smart Cards, Holographic Storage, Storage Systems for Large Computer Systems (home servers or media servers).
2. Explore common cloud storage examples such as Google Drive, One Drive, etc.
3. Discuss the pros and cons of using cloud storage.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.2. Demonstrate the Use of the Desktop
indicator_description: Explore personalisation of the computer
examplars:
1. Change desktop icons.
2. Change mouse pointers.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: COMPONENTS OF COMPUTERS AND COMPUTER SYSTEMS
content_standard: B9.1.1.2. Demonstrate the Use of the Desktop
indicator_description: Identify and use file management techniques (drivers and hardware)
examplars:
1. Identify and explore the use of device drivers e. g. sound drivers and video graphic drivers
2. Explain plug-and-play devices.
3. Demonstrate how to install, update or delete drivers.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.1.2.1. Demonstrate the Use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Evaluate problems in the community that can be solvedwith technology
examplars: 1. Discuss problems in the community that can be solved using technology. Work in pairs.
core_competencies: CI, CC, CP, CC 8.1, CC 9.2, CP 5.1

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.1.2.1. Demonstrate the Use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Propose solutions to the problems identified
examplars: 1. Present technological solutions to the problems identified in B9.1.2.1.1.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.1.2.1. Demonstrate the Use of Technology in the Community
indicator_description: Design the solution selected
examplars:
1. Design the solution selected.
2. Present the solution designed.
NB: Tasks in B9.1.2.1 is to be done as a project work

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B9.1.3.1. Demonstrate How to Apply Health and Safety Measures in the Use ICT Tools
indicator_description: Evaluate health issues at workstations
examplars:
1. Discuss the use of ergonomic tools such as ergonomic keyboard and paperstand.
2. Demonstrate the appropriateness of the lighting system while working withthe computer.

subject: Computing
sub_strand: HEALTH AND SAFETY IN THE USE OF ICT TOOLS
content_standard: B9.1.3.1. Demonstrate How to Apply Health and Safety Measures in the Use ICT Tools
indicator_description: Evaluate Safety Risk Reduction at issues at workstations
examplars: 1. Discuss the heat generated by machines (e.g. computers, printers etc.) andhow to reduce excess heat.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add pictures, insert a screenshot and screen clipping and print screen
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the clip art, screenshot and screen clipping in the Insert Ribbon.
2. Demonstrate the use of the print screen key in capturing and inserting pictures.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of SmartArt
examplars: 1. Illustrate the use of SmartArt in the Illustrations group of the Insert Ribbon.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.1.1 Demonstrate How to Use Microsoft Word (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add Multimedia (audios, videos, animations), Charts and Hyperlinks
examplars: 1. Explore the use of the clip art and screenshot in the Insert Ribbon.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.2.1 Demonstrate How to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add pictures and insert screenshots
examplars: 1. Explore the use of the clip art and screenshot in the Insert Ribbon.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.2.1 Demonstrate How to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to animate slides in a presentation
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the use of transitions and animations.
2. Create a seven-slide presentation with animations and transitions.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.2.1 Demonstrate How to use Microsoft PowerPoint (Multimedia)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to add Multimedia (audios, videos etc.), tables and charts
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Insert Ribbon tab to add multimedia (e.g. audios, videos, etc.).
2. Demonstrate the use of tables and charts in slides.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.3.1. Critique a Desktop Published Document
indicator_description: Create and present a desktop published document (flyer, advertisement, invitation cards, business cards)
examplars: 1. Create and present a four-page document with images and overflow e.g.flyer, advertisement, storybook.
core_competencies: CI, CC, DL, CP, C15.4, DL 5.1, CC 9.6

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.3.1. Critique a Desktop Published Document
indicator_description: Describe a desktop published document
examplars:
1. Give out your document to another learner assigned to you and collect the learner's document.
2. Examine the document received in terms of the position of text and images, use of colour, mechanics, content accuracy, etc.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.3.1. Critique a Desktop Published Document
indicator_description: Evaluate a desktop published document
examplars: 1. Evaluate the assigned document using, but not limited to the following criteria: position of text and images, general layout of the document, use of colour, mechanics (punctuation, spelling, italics, capitalisation, etc.), andappropriateness of the design for the intended purpose.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.4.1. Demonstrate How to Use Spreadsheet (Advanced Operations)
indicator_description: Perform data filtering, sorting and validation
examplars:
1. Describe Fields (columns), Records (rows) and Tables (structured/ unstructured).
2. Illustrate examples of structured and unstructured tables.
3. Construct a structured data table of class members (e.g. data table may havethe following fields: Surname, First name, Date of Birth, Sex, Home Town, Region, etc).
4. Apply validation rules to check for errors.
5. Convert data tables to a list in MS Excel.
6. Demonstrate the process of entering data to a list.
7. Demonstrate the use of validation list in the Sex column (in Exemplar(s): 3)to provide options in a drop-down for data entry.
8. Demonstrate how to sort data in alphabetical order (ascending/descending)and filter data to display only selected data.
core_competencies: CC, DL, CP, DL 5.1, CP 5.1, CC 9.6

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.4.1. Demonstrate how to use spreadsheet (Advanced Operations)
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use styles, themes, templates and macros
examplars:
1. Describe the difference between templates and macros.
2. Create new spreadsheet documents from predefined templates in MS Excel.
3. Demonstrate the use of styles and themes on sample worksheets.
4. Do a project on formatting a dataset by applying styles and themes.
5. Explore the use of macros.
core_competencies: CI, CC, DL, CP, C15.4, DL 5.1, CC 9.6

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.2.4.1. Demonstrate how to use spreadsheet (Advanced Operations)
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of data tables, pivot tables, charts and pivot charts
examplars:
1. Explore pivot tables and charts.
2. Explore the use of a pivot table to display a summary of the dataset (referto indicator B9.2.4.1.1).
3. Insert a pivot chart to display the number of males and females in the class.
4. Demonstrate the use of the sort and filter features of the pivot table.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.1.1. Know the Concept of Computer Networking for Global Communications
indicator_description: Discuss types of e-commerce and the cashless society
examplars:
1. Explore the use of the Internet to engage in online business: selling, buyingand paying for products online.
2. Discuss the use of online banking systems (e.g. using mobile money, bitcoin, the use of MasterCard, Visa card, QR code payment system, etc.).

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.1.1. Know the Concept of Computer Networking for Global Communications
indicator_description: Justify eLearning potentials
examplars:
1. Explain the concept of eLearning, its benefits and disadvantages.
2. Discuss projects on a collaborative platform (e.g. iBox network, the use ofad hoc network to share resources, Wikis, Google Docs] etc.)

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.2.1 Demonstrate the Use of Social Networking and Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Identify the advantages and risks in the use of social media platforms
examplars:
1. Illustrate the benefits of using social media sites
2. Discuss the issues and risks surrounding the usage of social media platformsand how to avoid them.
3. Explore reply, reply all, forward and forward all features in the use of emails

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and the means of protection
indicator_description: Discuss cyberbullying, cyberstalking, digital footprint anddigital shadow on the Internet
examplars:
1. Watch a film or do a reading on cyberbullying, cyberstalking, digital footprint, digital shadows.
2. Discuss the nature of cyberbullying, cyberstalking, digital footprint and digital shadows.
3. Identify examples of cyberbullying, cyberstalking, digital footprint and digital shadows.
4. Discuss the effects on cyberbullying on individuals
core_competencies: CI, DI, CP, CC, DL 6.1, CP 6.4, CC 8.5

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.3.1. Recognise data threats and the means of protection
indicator_description: Explain ten (10) information hacking techniques on the Internet environment.
examplars:
1. Brainstorm information hacking techniques on the internet environment.
2. Explain ten (10) information hacking techniques e.g. phishing, keyloggers, Denial of Service attack, eavesdropping, etc.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.4.1 Demonstratethe Use of a Web Browser (Blogging)
indicator_description: Examine the importance of creating blogs
examplars: 1. Discuss the importance of creating blogs.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.3.4.1 Demonstratethe Use of a Web Browser (Blogging)
indicator_description: Develop a blog for the school or a social club
examplars:
1. Investigate the items to include in a school or social club blog.
2. Develop a blog for the school or a social club.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.1.1. Show an Understanding of the Concept of Programming
indicator_description: Describe the conversion of decimal into binary data typefor computer to recognise the meaning, process and store
examplars:
1. Convert decimal, binary and hexadecimal data from one format to another.
2. Show the results of calculating two or more binary numbers using the mathematical notation or operators in the number base two rule.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.1.1. Show an Understanding of the Concept of Programming
indicator_description: Identify the different tools which are accessible in Integrated Development Environment (IDE)
examplars:
1. Explore programming languages such as Snap, Scratch and Python to explainthe key terminologies (variables, operators, controls, events, etc.) around the coding environment.
2. Explore a web development programme to create a simple website.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.2.1. Analyse the Correct Step-by-step Procedure in Solving any Real-world Problem
indicator_description: Write a programme using flowchart and Pseudocode algorithm
examplars:
1. Discuss at least three ways to do proper hand-washing and prepare beverages with or without sugar and/or milk.
2. Write an algorithm for Exemplar(s): I that focuses on procedure correctness and shortest time to execute.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.2.1. Analyse the Correct Step-by-step Procedure in Solving any Real-world Problem
indicator_description: Translate a Flowchart algorithm to Pseudocode format andvice versa
examplars: 1. Write an algorithm using flowchart format. Convert or translate the same flowchart algorithm into a Pseudocode format.

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.3.1. Discuss Robot Intelligence Concepts
indicator_description: Construct a robot artefact using available lab componentsand tools
examplars:
1. Explain the three basic laws of robotics by Isaac Asimov i.e. Asimov's science fiction laws of robotics.
2. Demonstrate how a robot is assembled using real robots' toolkit/video/ pictures.
3. Explore a robotic software pack, e.g. Scratch, Webot, Snap, Mbot software, EV3 and Mobile Applications such as lightbot

subject: Computing
content_standard: B9.4.4.1 Discuss Artificial intelligence Concepts
indicator_description: Describe the knowledge-based systems (Expert systems) as the classical Artificial intelligence
examplars:
1. Illustrate the use of IF-THEN control structure for querying an expert system
2. Demonstrate how to input a request in any knowledge-based system to generate an output or result (e.g. Telemedicine system)
3. Demonstrate how to go onto the web and use Google's Teachable Machine demo
